node_pool_name.description: The name which should be used for the Kubernetes Node Pool. Contain only lowercase letters and numbers and be between 1 and 12 characters in length.

aks_cluster_resource_id.description: The resource ID of the AKS Cluster. Drag, drop and connect a AKS Cluster resource to automatically populate this field.

vm_size.description: The size of the Virtual Machine which should be used for the Node Pool.

node_count.description: The initial number of nodes which should exist in this Node Pool. If specified this must be between 1 and 1000 and between min_count and max_count.

node_labels.description: A mapping of Kubernetes Node Labels which should be applied to the Node Pool

node_taints.description: A list of Kubernetes taints which should be applied to the Node Pool. (e.g key=value:NoSchedule)

enable_auto_scaling.description: Should the Kubernetes Node Pool have auto scaling enabled?

min_count.description: The minimum number of nodes which should exist in this Node Pool when autoscaling is enabled.

max_count.description: The maximum number of nodes which should exist in this Node Pool when autoscaling is enabled.

mode.description: Should this Node Pool be used for System or User resources?. Possible values are System and User

spot_enabled.description: Should this Node Pool be a spot instance pool?

eviction_policy.description: The eviction policy for the Spot Node Pool. Possible values are Delete and Deallocate

spot_max_price.description: The maximum price you're willing to pay in USD per Virtual Machine. Valid values are -1 (the current on-demand price for a Virtual Machine) or a positive value with up to five decimal places.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.
